title: The Marketing Meeting
summary: Initial technical review and ICP scoring confirmed, with a significant disagreement over content validity and messaging authority, requiring content simplification and theme focus.

decisions.description: Ideal Customer Profile (ICP) groups identified using seed customers and defined by technology usage and staffing levels.
Campaign implementation simplified by foregoing video content initially.
Team to share 2-3 draft email themes for content modification.
Vinnie Micciche will provide finalized email content quickly to ensure accurate messaging.
Response time data point updated to 5.4 based on Vinnie Micciche's feedback.
Messaging should center on human involvement, AI, and personal response.
Michael Donovan to simplify deliverable to 2-3 cohorts with simple draft emails for refinement.

actionItems.task: Complete the current pipeline and scoring model.
Provide Vinnie Micciche and Jason Branham with two or three themes for weekly email campaigns.
Provide Michael Donovan with content for the email campaigns based on the themes.

tensions: Strong disagreement over content relevance and validity of current sales deck data.
Vinnie Micciche asserted messaging authority, demanding development halt until content needs are understood.
Vinnie Micciche expressed concern about Michael Donovan's focus on technical aspects over message substance.
Michael Donovan pushed back on Vinnie Micciche's rejection of 'economic buyer' targeting for email content.
referencesToPast: Historical revenue data originated from the invoice tracker.
Sales deck content was reliant on 'old BDC data' from ten years ago.
Response time data point updated to 5.4 based on Vinnie Micciche's previous feedback.
Cox study cited regarding 20% of salesperson's salary wasted on bad leads.
Internal company data shows about 30% of serviced leads are bad.

contributions.description: Provided an overview of the simplified dashboard, noting data origin and security.
Confirmed ICP groups developed by analyzing technology usage and staffing levels.
Requested a 60-90 second video featuring Vinnie Micciche and Jason Branham for campaign launch.
Expressed concern that sales deck content was outdated 'old BDC data' and lacked AI focus.
Asserted that messaging should come from industry experts who talk to dealers daily.
Stressed importance of messaging centered on human involvement, AI, and personal response.
Argued current materials fail to highlight company's deep utilization of real AI and conversational data layer.
Warned about dangers of fragmented AI tools and shared an anecdote about an illegal 'straw purchase'.
Reinforced value proposition by citing a Cox study on wasted leads.
Proposed a simplified approach for initial campaign launch, foregoing video and sharing draft emails.
Recommended Michael Donovan simplify deliverable to 2-3 cohorts with simple draft emails for refinement.
